configuration.cannotSaveDetails: There was an error trying to save configuration to the server. Please reload the page or try again later.

systemConfiguration.cronEnable: Long-running tasks will be processed automatically in the background. If disabled, you will need to set up a cron task on your server.
systemConfiguration.cronPerPagesCaption: Change how many site pages should be displayed between each triggered background processing run.
systemConfiguration.cronKeyCaption: Secret key to use if setting up a cron task on your server. Use the copy button to get the cron job URL.
systemConfiguration.copyCronUrl: Click this button to copy the full cron job URL to your clipboard.
systemConfiguration.loggingLevelCaption: Select how much details should be included in logs.
systemConfiguration.loggingLevelsWarningDetails: This can store a lot of data if left enabled for too long. Change this under Configuration | System.
systemConfiguration.themeCaption: Pick a light or dark global color theme.
systemConfiguration.accentColorCaption: Pick the main color used to accentuate important information.
systemConfiguration.expandHelpSystemEnabled: Help texts and buttons are displayed
systemConfiguration.expandHelpSystemCaption: If enabled, short help texts and full help buttons will be displayed next to each configuration option. If disabled, only a small icon is shown.
systemConfiguration.uninstallRemoveAllDataCaption: If enabled, all information stored by 4SEO to the database, including page data and any configuration, will be removed if you uninstall 4SEO. This cannot be reverted. No data will be removed immediately.
systemConfiguration.uninstallRemoveAllDataEnabled: All data will be removed when uninstalling
systemConfiguration.uninstallRemoveAllDataDisabled: Data will not be removed when uninstalling
systemConfiguration.deleteAllDataConfirmed: Next time 4SEO is uninstalled, all data stored in the database, including all configuration, will be deleted.
systemConfiguration.updateKeyCaption: Enter your update key to allow one-click updates using the Joomla! updater.
systemConfiguration.updateKeyObtain: Get this key from your Weeblr.com dashboard.
systemConfiguration.purgeErrorsAfterCaption: Choose how long error records will be kept before automatic deletion.
systemConfiguration.resetRemindersCaption: Click this button so that all messages where you pressed the "Remind me later" button are reset and displayed again.
systemConfiguration.canAutoClearNotificationsCaption: If set to Yes, pop-up notifications such as error or confirmation messages will disappear after a delay. You can still use the Pause button in the notification to stop the automatic closing.

extensionsConfiguration.toConfigureCaption: Select the extension for which you want to configure 4SEO behavior.
extensionsConfiguration.autoRedirectToJoomlaCaption:
If switching from sh404SEF/4SEF URLs to Joomla SEF URLs, page addresses generated by sh404SEF should be redirected to their Joomla counterpart so that search engines find them. 4SEO will do that automatically when this option is enabled.
Requires that the sh404SEF URLs database table is still present on the server, even if sh404SEF was uninstalled.
extensionsConfiguration.enableStructuredData: Generate Structured data for product pages
extensionsConfiguration.enableStructuredDataCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will automatically generate Product structured data on product pages, and more if possible. You can disable this feature if you prefer to use another solution to generate structured data for this extension.
extensionsConfiguration.enableExtensionStructuredDataCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will automatically generate Structured data for some or all of this extension pages.

integrations.gscEnabledCaption: Enable Google Search Console usage throughout 4SEO. When enabled, more controls are displayed to connect to your account and authorize access. If disabled, connection information are not lost if you already authorized access to your account.
integrations.connectOnUnsecureSite: 4SEO can only talk to Google Search Console over a secure, HTTPS connection. Please enable HTTPS on your site and make sure to use the HTTPS version of your admin to connect to Google Search Console.
integrations.gscConnectedTo: You are connected for the %1 property with access to the following services:
integrations.gscConnectionLost: Looks like connection to Google Search Console was lost. Please disconnect using the Disconnect your account button below, then perform the connection process again.
integrations.gscConnectedFeatures: A Search Console section is now available on your dashboard, and the various SEO Insights reports pages are ready to analyze and help you use your search results data. Look them up under the SEO Insights menu item.
integrations.gscNotConnected: Not connected yet, you won't be able to access data from Google Search Console
integrations.toConfigureCaption: 4SEO can integrate and retrieve data from various services. All services require that you have an account with them. After that, you can configure your connection here.
integrations.noIntegrationSelected: No integration selected, please select one in the list
integrations.googleConnectionConnectedCaption: Click to disconnect from your account. 4SEO won't be able to access data any longer.
integrations.googleConnectionDisconnectedCaption: Click to connect and authorize 4SEO to read data from Google using your account. You can disconnect at any time, either from 4SEO or in your Google account.
integrations.selectPropertyAfterAuth: Search Console account connected! Select a property to use with this website, or add this site as a property:
integrations.selectExistingPropertySC: You can use one of the already registered properties in this Search Console account:
integrations.gscPropertiesCaption: Select which property to use when reading data about this site from Google Search Console. Only properties you have full access to are listed.
integrations.gscPropertiesShouldCreate: This website should be added to Google Search Console if it cannot use an existing property.
integrations.gscNoProperty: Could not find any property associated with your account, or you do not have sufficient access permissions to use them. We can try to add this website, or maybe disconnect and try another account?
integrations.canAddSiteToSC: This website - %1 - has not been added to Google Search Console yet. Do you want to add, verify and use it automatically now?
integrations.canVerifySiteToSC: This website - %1 - has been added to Google Search Console but is not verified yet. Do you want to verify and use it now?
integrations.siteVerifyOnlineSC: For verification to work, the site must be available online (ie. not on your local machine), without password protection.
integrations.cannotVerifySC: The %1 property is not verified yet, but we cannot verify it for you.
integrations.cannotVerifyDomainLevel: It is a domain-level property, which can only be verified with the DNS method.
integrations.cannotVerifyUrlPrefix: We can only verify automatically the website 4SEO is running on.
integrations.shouldVerifyManually: You should verify it manually yourself before using it, or select another property.
integrations.addingAndVerifyingPropertySC: Adding and verifying %1 with Google Search Console
integrations.siteaAddedAndVerified: Website added and verified. Click to complete
integrations.gscSiteVerifiedDetails:
Google Search Console has started collecting information about the site performance in search results.
It may take a few days before any actual data shows up, so please be patient.
You can also check your data status directly at the Google Search Console website.
integrations.proxyRequiresActiveSub: To use Weeblr one-click Google Search Console authorization server, you need an active 4SEO subscription. Make sure to have your update key set under Configuration | System | Update key
integrations.gscViewFullResultsTitle: Open full results page on Google Search Console website (new window).
integrations.gscViewFullResults: View full results at Search Console
integrations.gscVerdictNeutral: URL is not known to Google
integrations.gscReferringUrls: URLs that link to this page
integrations.gscSelectDimension: No data to display. Select one or more dimensions to show by clicking buttons for Clicks, Impressions, etc
integrations.gscEnabledDimensions: Dimensions are information on how your website appears in Google: how many clicks, how many times displayed (impressions), average position, etc.
integrations.gscReportNotConnected: Not connected to Google Search Console
integrations.gscReportsShouldConnect: SEO Insights reports are created with data from the Google Search Console. The Search Console provides information on how your website pages are displayed and clicked on Google search results.
integrations.gscReportsNotConnected: You are currently not connected, or you have disabled the 4SEO Search Console integration.
integrations.gscReportsHowToConnect:
Create an account at the Google Search Console website if you do not have one already.
With an account, you can then connect this site to the Search Console in a few clicks directly from inside 4SEO.
Using Search Console is entirely free.
integrations.gscCannibalizationReportDesc: The Cannibalization report shows keywords for which multiple pages of your site are displayed in Google search results.
integrations.gscKwOpportunitiesReportDesc: The Keywords opportunities report shows keywords and pages combinations where optimization work could yield increased traffic from Google search results.
integrations.gscCannibKeywordTitle: Click to view pages cannibalizing each other for keyword %1
integrations.gscSeveralPagesForKw: Several pages are displayed in Google search results for this keyword.
integrations.gscCannibPagesPopup: This may indicate some of your site pages are competing with each other which is often negative. Use this report to ensure pages content match the desired keywords and reinforce each other.
integrations.gscPagesOpportunitiesReportDesc: The Pages opportunities report shows pages and keywords combinations where optimization work could yield increased traffic from Google search results.
integrations.gscPagesOppPage: Keywords for page %1
integrations.gscPagesOppKeywordsPopupTitle: This page is displayed in Google search results for multiple keywords
integrations.gscPagesOppPageTitle: Click to view keywords associated with page <%1>
integrations.gscPagesOppKeywordsPopup: This may indicate this page content is not focused on the desired keywords. Use this report data to refine the page content, ensuring it matches wanted keywords.
integrations.gscOppMultipleKeywordsDesc: This page ranks for multiple keywords. This may indicate the page content is not focused on the desired keywords. Use this report data to refine the page content, ensuring it matches wanted keywords.
integrations.gscHiImpLowPosDesc: This page is displayed often in Google Search results but on the second or more page.
integrations.gscHiClicksLowPosDesc: This page gets many clicks despite being displayed on the second or more page of Google Search Results.
integrations.gscOppHintDesc: With some work on content, internal ranking and other techniques, it may be possible to take advantage of the high number of impressions to increase traffic to the site.
integrations.gscKeywordsForPageDesc: This page is showing in Google Search Results for the keywords listed below.
integrations.gscPagesForKeywordDesc: The pages listed below are showing in Google Search Results for this keyword.
integrations.gscKwOppKeywordTitle: Click to view pages associated with keyword <%1>
integrations.gscHiImpLowPosKwDesc: Pages listed below are displayed often in Google Search results for this keyword but on the second or more page.
integrations.gscHiClicksLowPosKwDesc: Pages listed below get many clicks for this keyword despite being displayed on the second or more page of Google Search Results.
integrations.gscDataLoadingError: Unable to load data from Google Search Console API. Maybe try a bit later, or disconnect and reconnect your account. More information is likely available on your server, in 4SEO log files.
integrations.mismatchedPropertySC: The main address of this site is %1 but you are trying to connect to the Search Console with the site loaded at %2. To connect to the Search Console, please use the main address of the site.

insightsSettings.title: SEO Insights settings
insightsSettings.gscKwExclusionsCaption: Keywords listed here will be excluded from any data used in SEO Insights reports. List one keyword per line. Keyword case does not matter and they can have multiple words, for instance bicyCLE or mountain bikes
insightsSettings.gscPagesExclusionsCaption: Pages URLs listed here will be excluded from any data used in SEO Insights reports.
insightsSettings.gscPagesUrlSpec: One URL per line, should start with a /.
insightsSettings.gscCanIncludeWildcards: You can use wildcards: {*} and {?}.
insightsSettings.gscInsightsMinClicksCaption: Any Search console record with less than this number of clicks in Google search results will not be taken into account in SEO Insights reports.
insightsSettings.gscInsightsMinImpressionsCaption: Any Search console record with less than this number of impressions in Google search results will not be taken into account in SEO Insights reports. An impression is the fact a page is displayed in Google search results when someone makes a search.
insightsSettings.gscInsightsMaxKwLengthCaption: Any Search console record related to a keyword longer than this will not be taken into account in SEO Insights reports. Google users may search for very long phrase, for instance an entire error message. Keeping these in reports is not useful as they are exceptionals.
insightsSettings.gscOppMultipleKwPerPageCaption: The minimum number of keywords for which a page shows up in Google Search Results to be considered as an opportunity

socialnetworksConfiguration.ogpEnabledCaption: If enabled, Open Graph Protocol codes will be inserted into pages to facilitate sharing them on social networks, particularly Facebook.
socialnetworksConfiguration.tCardsEnabledCaption: If enabled, Twitter Cards codes will be inserted into pages to facilitate sharing them on X (Twitter).
socialnetworksConfiguration.tCardsTypeCaption: Select your preferred Twitter Cards type used when sharing over Twitter.
socialnetworksConfiguration.tCardsSiteAccountCaption: Enter the X (Twitter) account associated with this website, including the initial @ sign
socialnetworksConfiguration.tCardsCreatorAccountCaption: Enter the X (Twitter) account associated with the content author, including the initial @ sign. Can be empty in most cases.
socialnetworksConfiguration.twitterAccountSpec: Should start with @, without any space.
socialnetworksConfiguration.defaultImageCaption: Enter the URL or select a default image for pages that do not have any. It will be used when sharing that page on social networks.

robotstxtConfiguration.settingTitleCaption: The robots.txt file, located at the root of your site tells complying search engines which pages they can or cannot crawl. It also tells them the location of your sitemap, if you have one.

customSitemapConfiguration.settingTitleCaption: The sitemap-4seo-custom.txt file, located at the root of your site, can be used to manually add pages to your xml content sitemap. Enter only fully qualified URLs (similar to https://www.example.com/some-nice-article), one per line.

ui.confirmBeforeCloseHelp: There are some unsaved changes, they will be lost if you close without saving.

pagesSettings.title: Pages settings
pagesSettings.insertAutoCanonicalCaption: If enabled, whenever possible, 4SEO will automatically insert a canonical link in non-canonical pages.
pagesSettings.insertAutoSelfCanonicalCaption: If enabled, a self-referencing canonical link will also be inserted on canonical pages.
pagesSettings.useCanonicalFallbackStrategy: Find canonical by removing query string
pagesSettings.useCanonicalFallbackStrategyCaption: If enabled and no canonical link exists for a page, try building one by removing the query string. The query string is the part of a URL after the ?. Has no effect if the URL does not have a query string. Handle with care, may not work on all sites.
pagesSettings.requiresFullCrawl: Requires that a full crawl has completed.
pagesSettings.metaAutoDescIfMissingCaption: If enabled, whenever possible, 4SEO will automatically compute and insert a meta description tag for pages which do not have one already.
pagesSettings.collectionEnabledCaption: If disabled, 4SEO will stop recording information about existing and new pages on the site.
pagesSettings.collectIncoming404sCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will record 404 errors caused by external requests, eg robots or bad links from other sites. Most of these requests are not important and waste space. 404s caused by broken links on your site will always be collected.
pagesSettings.collectApplyRobotsTxtCaption: If enabled, pages that are excluded in your robots.txt file will not be analyzed.
pagesSettings.collectApplyNoIndexCaption: If enabled, pages that are excluded with a noindex meta tag will not be analyzed.
pagesSettings.collectApplyNoFollowCaption: If enabled, links found on pages with a nofollow meta tag will not be analyzed.
pagesSettings.collectSkipNoFollowCaption: If enabled, links found in your pages with rel attribute containing nofollow will not be analyzed.
pagesSettings.collectionResetCaption: Restart a new analysis of your site pages.
pagesSettings.collectionResetCaptionDeletedData: Previously collected pages data is deleted. Custom information you may have created is preserved.
pagesSettings.collectionExclusionsCaption: Enter a URL specification to exclude some URLs from collection, one per line.
pagesSettings.collectionInclusionsCaption: Enter a URL specification to include some URLs into collection, one per line.
pagesSettings.collectionUrlSpecCaption: You can use {*} and {?} wildcards. Start with a / and no space. Do not include initial /index.php when not using URL rewriting on the site.
pagesSettings.collectionDomainsCaption: Enter a list of other domains that should not be analyzed, one per line.
pagesSettings.collectionDomainsSpecCaption: Enter only the domain name, eg weeblr.com or www.weeblr.com. No http://, no space and no /
pagesSettings.basicAuthCaption: If you have password-protected the site, enter below you access identifier and password so that our crawler can analyze your pages.
pagesSettings.crawlerEnableCertsCheckCaption: If enabled, TLS (or SSL) certificates will be validated before accessing pages of the site. Disable this when developing with self-signed certificates, on localhost for instance.
pagesSettings.crawlerSuggestDisableCertsCheck:
4SEO cannot load pages of your site. A common reason is an invalid TSL (SSL) certificate. It can happen when developing with self-signed certificates, on localhost for instance.
Please disable TLS certificates checks from Pages Settings (Site Analysis tab) or use a valid TLS certificate.
pagesSettings.crawlerBypassExternalCacheCaption: You may need to enable this if you use a CDN or a server-level cache system to cache your site entire pages and 4SEO analysis cannot analyse pages. You may need additional configuration of your caching service. Pleaser refer to the 4SEO help for more details.
pagesSettings.crawlerSuggestBypassExternalCache:
It looks like your site, or some pages of your site, are cached by an external caching system such as a CDN (Cloudflare, KeyCDN, etc).
If 4SEO cannot analyze any page, enable External cache bypass under Pages | Settings | Site Analysis to work around this.
pagesSettings.crawlerSuggestHtPassword:
4SEO cannot load pages of your site. A common reason is if the site is password protected.
If so, make sure to enter your id and password in Pages Settings (Site Analysis tab)
pagesSettings.canonicalRootUrlCaption: Enter the main website address if 4SEO did not guess it right. Include https or http, and whether you use the www prefix or not.
pagesSettings.enforceCanonicalRootUrl: Always redirect to site main address
pagesSettings.enforceCanonicalRootUrlCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will redirect requests made to other addresses for this site to the canonical one entered above. For instance, from https://example.com to https://www.example.com.
pagesSettings.perfMeasurementEnabled: Collect Core Web Vitals performance information
pagesSettings.perfMeasurementEnabledCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will collect real-world performance information from the visits to your site, exactly as Google does in its Core Web Vitals specification.
pagesSettings.perfMeasurementResetCaption: Performance data is accumulated over about a month, according to Core Web Vital specification. Reset performance data to clear all previously recorded data. For instance, if you made some changes you want to test.
pagesSettings.perfDataExclusionsCaption: Enter a URL specification to exclude some URLs from performance measurement, one per line.
pagesSettings.perfDataInclusionsCaption: Enter a URL specification to include some URLs from performance measurement, one per line.
pagesSettings.perfProbePerPagesCaption: Enter the number of pages viewed on your site between 2 consecutive measurements.
pagesSettings.perfProbeIncreaseCaption: On busy sites, increase the number to avoid 4SEO taking too many measurements. On sites with few visitors, reduce the number to have enough measurements.
pagesSettings.perfProbePerPagesSpec: Enter a number of pages between 1 and 10,000
pagesSettings.perfProbeTimeBetweenDataPointsCaption: Enter the minimal time to wait between 2 consecutive measurements for the same page.
pagesSettings.perfProbeTimeBetweenDataPointsSpec: Enter a number of minutes between 1 and 180.
pagesSettings.enforceLowerCaseUrlsCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will redirect requests made to URLs with uppercase letters to the lowercase version of the URL. For instance, from https://example.com/MyPage to https://example.com/mypage. This option has no effect if 4SEF or sh404SEF are running on the site, as these already ensure the proper URL case is used.
pagesSettings.metaAutoDescRecommendedLengthCaption: Your preferred meta description length when auto-generating the description. Also used in the SERP preview display.

editing.frontendEnabledCaption: If enabled, users can modify the most important page meta data from the frontend.
editing.frontendPermissionsCaption: To allow frontend editing for a user group, assign the %1 permission to that group in Joomla permissions settings for 4SEO.

pages.statusDuplicateMeta: This page %1 is duplicated on %2 pages
pages.resetRequired: Any setting change in this section will take effect only after a Reset analysis, under Pages | Settings | Site analysis, followed by a new full analysis.

sitemaps.enabledSection: Handle XML sitemap creation and updates
sitemaps.enabledCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will automatically create and update as needed an XML sitemap for your site.
sitemaps.addToRobotsCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will include a line in your robots.txt file to tell search engines where to find your sitemap.
sitemaps.pingSelectCaption: Select which search engines 4SEO should submit new or updated XML sitemaps.
sitemaps.noPing: Do not ping any search engine
sitemaps.rebuildConfirm: Delete current %1 sitemap and start rebuilding a new one?
sitemaps.rebuildInProgressDetails: Sitemap is in progress, watch the sitemap dashboard for status.
sitemaps.rebuildPendingAnalysis: Site content analysis has not completed yet, the sitemap will be rebuilt automatically when analysis is done.
sitemaps.dashEnabled: Sitemap is automatically created and updated.
sitemaps.dashDisabled: Sitemap handling is disabled in Settings.
sitemaps.dashAddRobotsEnabled: Sitemap is automatically added to robots.txt.
sitemaps.dashAddRobotsDisabled: Sitemap is not added automatically to robots.txt
sitemaps.dashSeSubmitEnabled: Sitemap is automatically submitted to search engine(s).
sitemaps.dashSeSubmitDisabled: Sitemap is not automatically submitted to search engine(s)
sitemaps.dashSeTrackEnabled: Search Engines visits to sitemaps are recorded.
sitemaps.dashSeTrackDisabled: Search engines visits tracking is disabled in Settings.
sitemaps.resetRequired: Changing your robots.txt file content may affect which pages are analyzed and included in your sitemap. It is best to perform a Reset analysis, under Pages | Settings | Site analysis, followed by a new full analysis.

sitemapsSettings.title: Sitemaps settings
sitemapsSettings.exclusionsCaption: Enter a URL specification to exclude some URLs from the sitemap, one per line.
sitemapsSettings.inclusionsCaption: Enter a URL specification to include some URLs from the sitemap, one per line.
sitemapsSettings.imagesUrlSpecCaption: You can use {*} and {?} wildcards. Start with a / and no space.
sitemapsSettings.imageInclusionMinWidthCaption: Enter the minimum width for an image to be included in your sitemap.
sitemapsSettings.imageInclusionMinHeightCaption: Enter the minimum height for an image to be included in your sitemap.
sitemapsSettings.imageInclusionSpec: Specify the dimension as a number of pixels. Enter 0 to disable this check.
sitemapsSettings.collectImagesOnCategoriesCaption: When disabled, 4SEO will skip collecting images from Joomla content categories pages in the sitemap. Avoids images being listed once on the category page and once on the article page.
sitemapsSettings.refreshSitemapDelay: Minimal delay between consecutive sitemap updates
sitemapsSettings.refreshSitemapDelayCaption: The minimum time 4SEO should wait between rebuilding the sitemap after a change is detected on the site. Too frequent updates will consume resources, make the sitemap less available and should be avoided when pinging search engines with each new sitemap. Note that only really large sites should change from the default.
sitemapsSettings.gscActiveNeeded: Sitemaps can only be submitted to Google if the Google Search Console integration is enabled and active.

rulesSettings.title: SEO rules settings
rulesSettings.enabledCaption: When disabled, SEO rules will stop being applied - but all their configuration is preserved.
rulesSettings.runOnAfterRouteFirst: Check rules as early as possible
rulesSettings.runOnAfterRouteFirstCaption: Enable if using full page caching and some 4SEO rules are not executed. Full page caching can be from either Joomla Cache system plugin, or from a third-party plugin. If not using full page caching, leave disabled as there may be a small performance impact of enabling this feature. Requires Joomla 4 and up.

redirectsSettings.title: Redirects settings
redirectsSettings.enabledCaption: When disabled, redirects rules will stop being applied - but all their configuration is preserved.
redirectsSettings.aliasesEnabledCaption: When disabled, aliases entered for specific pages on the Pages page will stop being applied - but all their configuration is preserved.

replacerSettings.title: Replacer settings
replacerSettings.enabledCaption: When disabled, replacement rules will stop being applied - but all their configuration is preserved.

analyticsSettings.title: Analytics settings
analyticsSettings.enabledCaption: When disabled, analytics configurations will stop being applied - but they will all be preserved.

links.linkToErrorTitle: Some links on your site go to errors
links.linkToErrorMessage: %1 triggers an error when visited. You should fix that error or modify the links to it found on pages below to avoid your visitors seeing those errors.
links.linkToRedirectTitle: Avoid links with redirects in your content
links.linkToRedirectMessage: Pages listed below have links to %1. You should replace them with direct links to the final URL, %2.

errors.siteAddressMismatch: Using address %1 but site main address is set to %2. See documentation

rules.noRulePrompt: Press New in the toolbar to add one.

ruleEdit.title: Name
ruleEdit.titleCaption: Enter a name for the rule. Only for your reference.
ruleEdit.titleSearchCaption: Title is the only searchable field, so make sure it represents the rule well.
ruleEdit.orderAfterCaption: Select a rule to place the current rule after. Or choose First to have the rule executed first.
ruleEdit.orderAfterNoItemSelected: No selection, rule we be added at the end of the list.
ruleEdit.notesCaption: Enter any notes you want to keep about this rule. Only for your reference.
ruleEdit.enabledCaption: Choose to always disable, always enable or enable with some conditions this rule. Possible conditions will appear when you select "With some conditions"
ruleEdit.urlSpecCaption: Enter one or more URLs where the rule should run (one per line).
ruleEdit.urlNegSpec: Do NOT run on this/these page(s)
ruleEdit.urlNegSpecCaption: Enter one or more URLs where the rule should not run (one per line).
ruleEdit.urlSpecSpec: Start with /. {*} represents any number of any character. {?} represents any single character. /{*} means all pages of the site.
ruleEdit.urlSpecSpecMultiple: Wildcards ({*} and {?}) or regular expressions ({~}) cannot be used if entering multiple URLs.
ruleEdit.disregardQueryCaption: Whether to include the query string (variables after the path such as ?fbclid=123456) when comparing the URLs
ruleEdit.disregardQueryDisabled: Query string will be taken into account
ruleEdit.disregardCaseCaption: Whether to consider characters case when comparing the URLs. If enabled, /page and /Page will be considered the same.
ruleEdit.disregardCaseDisabled: URL case will be taken into account
ruleEdit.includedLanguagesCaption: Select one or more languages in the list. The rule will run on any page for the selected languages.
ruleEdit.includedExtensionsCaption: Select one or more components in the list. The rule will run on any page for those components.
ruleEdit.includedCategoriesCaption: Select one or more categories in the list. The rule will run on any page for those categories.
ruleEdit.includedUsersGroupsCaption: Select one or more users groups in the list. The rule will only run on requests made by a user from one of these groups.
ruleEdit.excludedUsersGroups: Do not run for these users groups
ruleEdit.excludedUsersGroupsCaption: Select one or more users groups in the list. The rule will NOT run on requests made by a user from one of these groups.
ruleEdit.includedHomeAddressesCaption: Enter one or more website home addresses, one per line. The rule will only run on requests made by a visitor to one of these website home addresses.
ruleEdit.excludedHomeAddresses: Do not run for these home addresses
ruleEdit.excludedHomeAddressesCaption: Enter one or more website home addresses, one per line. The rule will NOT run on requests made by a visitor to one of these website home addresses.
ruleEdit.homeAddressSpec: Must start with https:// or http:// and end with a slash (/). Examples: https://www.example.com/, https://www.example.com/sub-folder/
ruleEdit.includedIpsCaption: Enter one or more IP addresses. The rule will only run on requests made from an IP address on the list
ruleEdit.excludedIps: Do not run for these IP addresses
ruleEdit.excludedIpsCaption: Enter one or more IP addresses. The rule will NOT run on requests made from an IP address on the list
ruleEdit.ipsSpec: Enter one IP address per line. Either v4 (12.456.78.98) or v6 (2e69:b3f1:d74f:6529:004e:dd80:2924:0f02) full addresses can be used. You can also use {*} and {?} wildcard characters.
ruleEdit.excludedCategories: Do not run for these categories
ruleEdit.excludedCategoriesCaption: Select one or more categories in the list. The rule will NOT run on any page for those categories.
ruleEdit.enableBetweenCaption: If a start and/or and end date is set, the rule will only be active between these dates.
ruleEdit.errorBetween: If both start and end dates are set, the Start date should be before the Stop date.
ruleEdit.actionsCaption: Select an action to perform when the rule is triggered.
ruleEdit.actionCanonicalTargetCaption: Should start with /, http:// or https://. Can be on the same site or another. Can include {*} and {?} wildcards as well, unless you specified multiple pages this rule applies to. /{*} means insert a canonical to itself.
ruleEdit.blockTypeCaption: If a visitor requests a page that matches this rule, they will not be able to access the page. They will see one of several possible error response.
ruleEdit.rawContentCaption: The content your enter here will be inserted in the page.
ruleEdit.rawContentSpecCaption: Content is inserted exactly as is, must be valid HTML/script/CSS.
ruleEdit.rawContentTotalTooLong: Combined length of all raw content fields should not exceed %1 characters.
ruleEdit.redirectActionsCaption: Select a type of redirect to use when the rule is triggered. Redirecting to SEF only applies to requests for a non-SEF URL.
ruleEdit.actionRedirectTargetCaption: Should start with /, http:// or https://. Can be on the same site or another. Can include {*} and {?} wildcards as well, unless you specified multiple URLs to redirect from.
ruleEdit.actionReappendQueryCaption: Whether to put back the query string (variables after the path such as ?fbclid=123456) on to the final URL when it was disregarded initially.
ruleEdit.actionReappendQueryEnabled: Query string will be appended to final URL
ruleEdit.actionReappendQueryDisabled: Query string will not be appended to final URL
ruleEdit.replacerActionsCaption: With text, your source will be replaced with the target. With link, the source will be replaced with a link to your target.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerSourceCaption: Enter the text you want to replace. Can include HTML markup.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerProtectLinksCaption: If enabled, replacement will not happen inside of html href and src attributes.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerProtectLinksEnabled: Addresses in links will not be replaced.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerProtectLinksDisabled: Addresses in links will be replaced.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerProtectHnTagsCaption: If enabled, replacement will not happen inside of html title tags such as H1 or H2.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerProtectHnTagsEnabled: Content in title tags will not be replaced.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerProtectHnTagsDisabled: Content in title tags will be replaced.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerLocation: Where to look for that text?
ruleEdit.actionReplacerLocationCaption: Whether to look for that text anywhere on the page or only in specific areas.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerMaxReplacementsCaption: Enter a number here to limit how many replacements to perform on a page. Only the first occurences up to that number will be replaced. Useful when auto-linking keywords to pages as you do not want too many of the same link on the same page.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerMaxReplacementsSpec: Enter a round number only. Entering zero basically disables the rule.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerCaseSensitiveCaption: Enable to restrict search to exact same letter case.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerCaseSensitiveEnabled: Replace only if same letters case
ruleEdit.actionReplacerWholeWordsOnlyCaption: Restrict search to entire words only. If enabled, searching for 'test' will find 'This is a test.' but not 'We are testing.'.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerWholeWordsOnlyDisabled: Replace even if part of a word.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerNoFollowCaption: If enabled, a nofollow attribute will be added to the links created with this rule.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerNoFollowEnabled: nofollow attribute will be added to links.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerNoFollowDisabled: Links will be created without a nofollow attribute.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerTargetBlankCaption: If enabled, all links created by this rule will open in a new browser tab or window.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerTargetBlankEnabled: Created links will open in new tab
ruleEdit.actionReplacerTargetBlankDisabled: Created links will stay in same tab.
ruleEdit.actionReplacerTargetCaption: Which replacement to use. Can be some text with HTML. If you choose to replace with a link, enter a URL here.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsProviderCaption: Select the Analytics provider to use when inserting the code snippet.
ruleEdit.selectAnalyticsProvider: Select an Analytics provider in the list below
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsIdCaption: The Google Analytics property id for your web site. Look for it in your Google Analytics account.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsGaAnonymizeCaption: When enabled, visitors IP address will not be collected by Google Analytics.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsGaDisplayFeaturesCaption: If enabled, the display features plugin will be added to the tracking snippet. It is required to use remarketing and demographic data in Google Analytics.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsGaAdFeaturesCaption: If enabled, the Advertising features plugin will be added to the tracking snippet.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsGaLinkAttributionCaption: If enabled, the Enhanced Links attribution plugin will be added to the tracking snippet.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsGtmIdCaption: The Google Tag Manager ID for your site. Look for it in your Google Tag Manager account.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsGtmDatalayerCaption: An optional datalayer for the Google Tag manager Container. Inserted as is. Similar to var dataLayer = [{'pageCategory':'signup', 'visitorType': 'AllVisitorsAreImportant'}];
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsFbpixelIdCaption: The Facebook Pixel ID for your site. Look for it in your Facebook for Business dashboard.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsFbpixelIdSpec: A series of digits similar to 374368950985262.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsMatomoIdCaption: The site ID for your site. Look for it in your Matomo provider websites list.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsMatomoIdSpec: A number such as 2, 11 or 12345.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsMatomoEndpointCaption: The full URL of your Matomo server. Similar to https://matomo.org/.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsMatomoTrackWithoutJsCapti: If enabled, an image link will be added on top of regular Javascript measurement. See Matomo documentation for details.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsClarityIdCaption: The Clarity Project ID for your site. Look for it in your Clarity dashboard.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsClarityIdSpec: A series of numbers and letters similar to 93uycfg258.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsCloudflareIdCaption: The Cloudflare Token for your site. Look for it in your Cloudflare dashboard.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsCloudflareIdSpec: A series of numbers and letters similar to 7dbd072146354d41a89da4436966ffb4.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsFathomIdCaption: The Fathom Analytics ID for your site. Look for it in your Fathom Analytics dashboard.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsFathomIdSpec: A series of letters similar to ABCDEFG.
ruleEdit.actionAnalyticsCustomCaption: Any custom code your provide here will be inserted with the provider dedicated code. Use this to provide custom values to the provider.
ruleEdit.sdRuleLegend: Structured data will be included in the pages this rule applies to. Select first the data type then let data be found automatically or provide your own.
ruleEdit.sdRuleTypeCaption: Select one of the supported Structured data types to apply when the rule is ran.
ruleEdit.sdCustomCodeCaption: Enter raw structured data you want to include in the data record. Will be inserted exactly as is, make sure to check and validate the final output in the page.
ruleEdit.sdHeadlineCaption: Enter a headline to use for this content. When Automatic and missing, defaults to the page title.
ruleEdit.sdDescriptionCaption: Enter a description to use for this content. When Automatic and missing, defaults to the page meta description.
ruleEdit.sdAuthorCaption: Enter the full name of this content author. When Automatic, defaults to using the default Person in Settings.
ruleEdit.sdImageLabel: Image to use in structured data
ruleEdit.sdImageCaption: Enter the URL or select an image. When Automatic, defaults to using the current page image or sharing image.
ruleEdit.sdPublisherCaption: Enter the full name of this content publisher. When Automatic, defaults to using the default Person in Settings.
ruleEdit.sdDatePublishedCaption: Enter the content first publication date. Time is optional and uses your browser time zone.
ruleEdit.sdDateModifiedCaption: Enter the content last modification date. Time is optional and uses your browser time zone.
ruleEdit.sdAggregateRatingCaption: Enter the values defining this content rating.
ruleEdit.sdRatingValueCaption: The rating value for this rule items. Must be a number, possibly with a decimal part (eg: 2.5 or 3.75), and between the worst and best values entered below.
ruleEdit.sdRatingCountCaption: Total count of ratings taken into account when computing the rating above.
ruleEdit.sdWorstRatingCaption: The lowest possible value for a rating.
ruleEdit.sdBestRatingCaption: The highest possible value for a rating.
ruleEdit.sdVideoNameCaption: Enter the full name of the video.
ruleEdit.sdThumbnailUrlCaption: The full URL of a thumbnail image representing the video (BMP, GIF, JPEG, PNG, WebP or SVG)
ruleEdit.sdContentUrlCaption: The URL of the video file.
ruleEdit.sdUploadDateCaption: Date and optionally time when the video was first uploaded.
ruleEdit.sdCourseNameCaption: Enter the full title for this course. Defaults to the article or page title.
ruleEdit.sdCourseModeCaption: How and where this course is provided. If In-person or Both, you must also provide a location below.
ruleEdit.sdCourseScheduleError: You must provide a Course Schedule OR a Course Workload. A Course Schedule requires both a Repeat Count and a Repeat Frequency.
ruleEdit.sdCourseWorkloadCaption: The expected workload for this course. This field represents the total time to watch all videos or complete all assignments and exams for the course. %1 Example: PT1H for one hour, P1D for one day.
ruleEdit.sdCourseScheduleCaption: Only one of Course Schedule or Course Workload properties is required. Google recommends the Course Schedule if you want to highlight pacing (for example, 4 hours per week), or start and end dates. Otherwise, Course Workload works for most cases.
ruleEdit.sdCourseRepeatCountCaption: The number of times the course is repeated. Used together with the Repeat Frequency field and Duration. For instance, setting Repeat Count to 4 and Repeat Frequency to Monthly means the course lasts for four months.
ruleEdit.sdCourseRepeatFrequencyCaption: The frequency unit to describe how often the course repeats. Used together with the Repeat Count field.
ruleEdit.sdCourseDurationCaption: The suggested pacing for how long the average user can expect to spend in duration of the course, in Repeat Frequency units. %1 Example: If duration is PT4H, for four hours and Repeat Frequency is Monthly, then user should expect to spend 4 hours per month on the course. Don't specify the entire length of the course with this property. Use the Course Workload field instead.
ruleEdit.sdCourseStartDateCaption: The day the course starts, if applicable.
ruleEdit.sdCourseIso8601Format: Use the 8601 duration format.
ruleEdit.sdCourseEndDateCaption: The day the course ends, if applicable.
ruleEdit.sdCourseInstructorNameCaption: Enter the full name of the course instructor. This must be a Person.
ruleEdit.sdCourseInstructorDescriptionCaption: Credentials and other information about the instructor.
ruleEdit.sdCourseOfferCategoryCaption: The total price to complete the course, including service fees. Don't list a price that is only eligible for some users (for example, 20% off for first time users).
ruleEdit.sdProviderCaption: Enter the full name of the organization that publishes this content. When Automatic, defaults to using the default organization in Structured data Settings.
ruleEdit.sdEventLocationNameCaption: Name for the location where the event takes place. Required unless the event is online only.
ruleEdit.sdEventLocationCaption: Location where the event takes place for and online or mixed event. Enter its full URL, starting with http:// or https://.
ruleEdit.sdEventLocationHelp: Google only accepts physical locations (ie: Offline) to trigger the enhanced Event experience in their search results. Online locations are technically valid but will not bring up rich results.
ruleEdit.sdEventLocationAddressCaption: Location where the event takes place for a in-person or mixed event. Enter its full physical address.
ruleEdit.sdEventNameCaption: Enter the full name for this event. Defaults to the article or page title.
ruleEdit.sdStartDateCaption: Start date and time of the event.
ruleEdit.sdEndDateCaption: End date and time of the event.
ruleEdit.sdOrganizerCaption: Enter the full name of the event organizer. Can be a person or an organization, if missing, defaults to using the default organization in Structured data Settings.
ruleEdit.sdPerformerCaption: Enter the full name of a person performing at the event. If missing, defaults to using the default person in Structured data Settings.
ruleEdit.sdPerformerTypeCaption: Select the performer type that best matches your event performer.
ruleEdit.sdEventAttendanceModeCaption: Whether the event is attended online, offline or a mixture of both.
ruleEdit.sdEventStatusCaption: The current event status. You can combine up to 2 statuses at the same time.
ruleEdit.sdEventPriceCaption: The lowest available price available, including service charges and fees.
ruleEdit.sdPriceSpec: Enter a number only, use one single . or , for decimal values.
ruleEdit.sdPriceValidUntilCaption: Date and time after which the price will not be available anymore.
ruleEdit.sdPriceCurrencyCaption: Select the currency used for your price.
ruleEdit.sdEventAvailabilityCaption: Whether tickets for the event can be obtained.
ruleEdit.sdEventValidFromCaption: The date and time at which tickets for your event can be purchased.
ruleEdit.sdOfferPriceCaption: The offer price of the product.
ruleEdit.sdAvailabilityCaption: Whether and how the product is available.
ruleEdit.sdOfferUrlCaption: Address of the web page where purchase takes place.
ruleEdit.sdSkuCaption: Your merchant-specific identifier for this product.
ruleEdit.sdBrandCaption: Full name of this product brand.
ruleEdit.sdItemConditionCaption: Select the option best describing the product condition, or enter a custom one.
ruleEdit.sdKeywordsCaption: Other terms for your recipe such as the season ("summer"), the holiday ("Halloween"), or other descriptors ("quick", "easy", "authentic"). Separate each keyword with ,
ruleEdit.sdRecipeCategoryCaption: The type of meal or course your recipe is about. For example: "dinner", "main course", or "dessert, snack".
ruleEdit.sdRecipeCuisineCaption: The region associated with your recipe. For example, "French", "Mediterranean", or "American".
ruleEdit.sdRecipeIngredientCaption: Ingredients used in the recipe. Include the quantities and put one ingredient per line.
ruleEdit.sdRecipeInstructionsCaption: Steps needed to prepare the recipe.
ruleEdit.sdRecipeYieldCaption: How many servings made out of this recipe. Must be a number if you specify calories for the recipe. Otherwise, you can also add a unit, eg "24 cookies"
ruleEdit.sdRecipePrepTimeCaption: The length of time it takes to prepare the dish. Always use in combination with Cook Time.
ruleEdit.sdRecipeCookTimeCaption: The time it takes to actually cook the dish. Always use in combination with Preparation Time.
ruleEdit.sdRecipeTotalTimeCaption: The total time it takes to prepare the cook the dish. Use either Total time or a combination of Preparation Time and Cooking Time.
ruleEdit.sdCaloriesCaption: The number of calories in each serving produced with this recipe. If you enter a number of calories, Quantity produced must be a number of servings.
ruleEdit.sdFaqModeCaption: How should FAQ elements be detected within your pages. Select CSS to use CSS selectors. Select Shortcodes if you wrap your questions and answers in {4seo_faq_question_start}, {4seo_faq_question_end} and {4seo_faq_answer_start}, {4seo_faq_answer_end} tags. Select Custom value to enter questions and answers here.
ruleEdit.sdFaqCssQuestionCaption: The exact CSS selector to look for which content will be used for questions. For instance: h2 or .question
ruleEdit.sdFaqCssAnswerCaption: The exact CSS selector to look for which content will be used for answers. For instance: h2 or .answer
ruleEdit.sdFaqQASpec: Question and answer cannot be empty. The question must be visible on the page. The answer can be hidden but visitors can view it with a click. Can contain some HTML tags.
ruleEdit.sdFaqCssSelectorCaption: Enter a CSS selector specification that identifies the desired HTML element(s). Typically an HTML tag or a CSS class or ID. For instance h2, h3 , .question, or #answer.
ruleEdit.faqPageCannotBeEmpty: A FAQ page question or answer cannot be empty.
ruleEdit.actionSmExcludeCaption: Exclude pages where this rule applies from the sitemap. When set to Yes, pages will always be excluded. If set to No, the pages can still be excluded for other reasons.
ruleEdit.actionSmExcludeAgeCaption: Exclude from sitemap if content has been modified or created more than the entered number of days. Leave at 0 to not test for content age.
ruleEdit.actionSmExcludeArchivedCaption: If enabled, archived content will be excluded from sitemap. An archived status exists for Joomla articles or categories but may not exist for other types of content.
ruleEdit.actionSmIncludeImagesCaption: If enabled, images identified during page analysis will be included in the sitemap to inform search engines of the most important images on your site.
ruleEdit.errorPageTitleCaption: The page header to display on this error page.
ruleEdit.errorPageTitleDefault: Oops! that page cannot be found...
ruleEdit.errorPageContentCaption: The main body text to display on this error page. Can contain raw HTML.
ruleEdit.errorPageContentDefault:
We do not have any page at the address you requested. Sorry about the trouble!

Please use the menu to navigate to other pages of the site, or perform a search to find what you're looking for!
ruleEdit.errorPageSuggestCaption: Whether 4SEO should try to offer a list of similar pages to the one that was requested but failed.
ruleEdit.actionErrorSuggestTitleCaption: A short text displayed before the suggested pages, if any. Can contain raw HTML.
ruleEdit.actionErrorSuggestTitleDefault: We do have these pages that look similar to your request, maybe try them first?
ruleEdit.actionErrorNoSuggestTextCaption: A short text displayed instead of suggested pages, if none are found. Can contain raw HTML.
ruleEdit.actionErrorMenuCaption: The error page is normally displayed using the modules and template associated with your home page. If not suitable, select here the existing menu item you want to use modules and template from.
ruleEdit.errorPageShowDetailsCaption: If enabled, the actual error code and error message will be displayed at the bottom of the error page.
ruleEdit.actionErrorCodeCaption: Select which specific error code this rule should apply to. Most often, you will use a custom error displayonly for 404: not found errors.
ruleEdit.cfSelector: Run based on custom field value
ruleEdit.cfSelectorCaption: Chose the custom field used to decide whether to run the rule.
ruleEdit.cfOperatorCaption: Select how to compare the custom field value and your desired value.
ruleEdit.cfValueCaption: Enter your desired value for the custom field, to trigger the rule.
ruleEdit.cfValue2Caption: Enter the maximum value for the custom field, when using the Between operator.
ruleEdit.useCustomFieldCaption: Select one of the custom fields defined on your site from the list. If that field has a value for a page, 4SEO will use that value instead of anything else.
ruleEdit.customValueMode: Click to switch between entering a custom value and selecting a custom field.
ruleEdit.productGlobalIdTypeCaption: Select one of the recognized global product id types. Note that ISBN is only valid for Books. Please refer to https://schema.org/Product for details.
ruleEdit.productGlobalIdCaption: Enter the actual global ID for this product, based on the global identifier type you selected above.
ruleEdit.actionMetaTitleSpecCaption: Customize the page title for pages affected by this rule. The current page title is represented with {page_title}. Add text after or before this tag, or replace it with something else entirely. You can also use any of the predefined 4SEO values, for instance: {page_title} - {4seo_site_name} to append the site name. Click the Help button below for a list of available tags.
ruleEdit.actionMetaDescSpecCaption: Customize the description meta tag for pages affected by this rule. The current meta description is represented with {page_description}. Add text after or before this tag, or replace it with something else entirely. You can also use any of the predefined 4SEO values, for instance: {page_description} - {4seo_site_name} to append the site name. Click the Help button below for a list of available tags.
ruleEdit.actionMetaDescIfEmptyCaption: Only apply this meta description format if the current page does not have a meta description. If 4SEO is set to generate a description automatically when none is present, this rule will have priority.
ruleEdit.actionMetaDescSuppressCaption: If enabled, 4SEO will remove the meta description from the page. Search results snippets are usually created automatically by search engines, and you may have better click rates with an empty description than by writing your own. You can still add a description for a specific page manually on the Pages page.
ruleEdit.actionMetaRobotsCustomCaption: Enter any valid meta robots tag value you want to use. It will be injected exactly as entered.
ruleEdit.forceDefaultImageCaption: If enabled, the default sharing image will be used on all pages to which this rule applies. The default sharing image is set under Configuration | Social networks
ruleEdit.actionReplacerSubLocation: Which metadata to look for that text?
ruleEdit.actionReplacerSubLocationCaption: Select which metadata to look for the text you want to replace. Replacing in Page title or Page description will also affect OpenGraph and Twitter Cards metadata.
ruleEdit.viewSpecCaption: After selecting components, you can restrict the rule to run only on some views of these components. Add one view per line. View names can only be a-z letters, numbers and underscores.
ruleEdit.viewNegSpec: Do NOT run for these views
ruleEdit.viewNegSpecCaption: After selecting components, you can prevent the rule to run on some views. Add one view per line. View names can only be a-z letters, numbers and underscores.
ruleEdit.sdProfileEntityTypeCaption: Select whether a Person or Organization represents best your profile.
ruleEdit.sdProfileNameCaption: The primary name under which the person or organization is identified. It is recommended to use here a real name.
ruleEdit.sdProfileAltNameCaption: An alternate public identifier, if applicable. For example, a social media handle if a person's real name is used in the Name field.
ruleEdit.sdProfilePageDescriptionCaption: The person or organization byline or credentials for the Profile page.
ruleEdit.sdSocialProfilesCaption: The URL to other external profiles or home pages for the profile, if applicable. Enter one fully qualified (starting with http:// or https://) URL per line.
ruleEdit.sdProfileImageCaption: The URL of a profile image, if applicable. If there are no images, don't include a default image, icon, or placeholder image in this field.
ruleEdit.sdProfileImageAdditionalCaption: URL of an additional profile image, if applicable. It is recommended to provide multiple high-resolution images (minimum of 50K pixels when multiplying width and height) with the following aspect ratios: 16x9, 4x3, and 1x1.
ruleEdit.sdProfileDateCreatedCaption: The date and time the profile was created, if applicable.
ruleEdit.sdProfileDateModifiedCaption: The date and time that information in the profile was last modified, if applicable. Should only represents human-edited metadata changes to the profile.
ruleEdit.sdMovieNameCaption: Enter the full name of the movie.
ruleEdit.sdMovieDirectorCaption: The full name of the director(s) of the movie. Separate multiple directors with a comma.
ruleEdit.sdMovieDateCreatedCaption: The date the movie was completed.
ruleEdit.sdMovieImageCaption: The URL of an image that represents the movie.
ruleEdit.sdMovieImageFormat: Must be vertical, high resolution, .jpg,.png or .gif, and as close as possible to a 6:9 aspect ratio
ruleEdit.ugaDeprecation: Google Universal Analytics will permanently stop operating on July 1st, 2024.
ruleEdit.ugaDeprecationSuggest: We suggest switching to another analytics tool as soon as possible.

crawl.enabled: Analysis is performed in the background
crawl.crawlFailure: Server cannot analyze the site, aborting.

sd.enabledCaption: If set to Yes, structured data will be added when possible to your site pages. Enable/disable them by type below. See help for details about structured data.
sd.enabledPerPageCaption: If set to Yes, structured data describing the content of individual pages will be added to your site pages when possible.
sd.enabledLocalBusinessCaption: If set to Yes, structured data describing your organization as a LocalBusiness will be added to your home page. Requires filling in business data on the Default Organization tab.
sd.enabledSiteLinksCaption: If set to Yes, structured data describing your search page will be added to your home page. This may allow users to search your site directly from search results.
sd.enabledBreadcrumbCaption: If set to Yes, structured data describing the path to the current page will be added to your site pages.
sd.enabledCleanupCaption: If set to Yes, existing structured data, which are usually invalid, will be removed from the page, or disabled, as much as can automatically be done.
sd.enabledBuiltInRulesCaption: If enabled, we will try to create Article structured data on all regular Joomla articles. If an mp4 video is detected in the content, VideoObject structured data will be added as well. We strongly recommend testing structured data output to be sure these rules fit your needs. Creating your own rules is likely to provide the best results for your site.

sdSettings.title: Structured data settings
sdSettings.businessTypeLegend: If this site is related to a business, enter its details here for inclusion in LocalBusiness structured data code. If not a business, leave the Type field empty but you can still fill-in the other details.
sdSettings.businessTypeCaption: Select one of the available business types. Leave empty if your organization is not a business.
sdSettings.personLegend: If content of this site is authored by a person, enter their details here for inclusion in Author structured data code. Only used when individual author details cannot be found.
sdSettings.personNameCaption: Enter the full name of the person, exactly as you want it to appear.
sdSettings.personUrlCaption: Enter a full URL related to the person, for instance their personal website or one of their social profile.
sdSettings.businessNameCaption: The full name of your organization. Cannot be empty.
sdSettings.businessUrlCaption: The full main URL for your organization website or online presence. Must start with https:// or http://.
sdSettings.organizationTelCaption: Full telephone number. Include the country code and the full number, must start with a + followed with a country code.
sdSettings.organizationPriceRangeCaption: The relative price range of a business, commonly specified by either a numerical range (for example, $10-15) or a normalized number of currency signs (for example, $$$). Must be less than 100 characters.
sdSettings.organizationStreetAddressCaption: Full street address, including street number
sdSettings.organizationPostalCodeCaption: Postal code in your local format.
sdSettings.organizationAddressRegionCaption: Local region your city is part of.
sdSettings.organizationAddressCountryCaption: Select the country where your organization is physically located or enter its full name.
sdSettings.organizationLogoCaption: Select or enter the address of your logo. Must be at least 112x112 pixels in size.
sdSettings.findCoordinatesCaption:
After entering organization location details, press this button to find its GPS coordinates, or manually enter latitude and longitude below.
Automatic coordinates retrieval may require that you set a Google Maps API key on the configuration tab.
sdSettings.organizationGeoLatitudeCaption: Enter your location latitude in numeric format, eg 1.2345. Must be between -90 and 90.
sdSettings.organizationGeoLongitudeCaption: Enter your location longitude in numeric format, eg 1.2345. Must be between -180 and 180.
sdSettings.googleMapsApiKeyCaption: Optional Google Maps API key. Needed for the Find coordinates and Show on a map buttons to work. Your key must have the Geocoding API and Maps Embed API access enabled.
sdSettings.organizationHoursTypeCaption: If your organization has opening hours, select one of the options. More options will appear based on your selection.
sdSettings.organizationHoursCustomCaption: Enter opening and closing hours. You can have one or two opening periods per day. If you are closed on a day, set it to both open and close at 00:00
sdSettings.organizationHoursHoursTypeCaption: Select one of the standard opening times, or use Specific hours in previous field for full customization.
sdSettings.hoursDayOpens2: Enter second %1 opening hour, if any.
sdSettings.hoursDayCloses2: Enter second %1 closing hour, if any.
sdSettings.sdRulesCountNoneButBuiltIn: No custom Structured data rule yet, but built-in rules are applied.
sdSettings.sdRulesCountNoneButBuiltInDisabled: No custom Structured data rule yet and built-in rules have been disabled.
sdSettings.sdProfileNameCaption: Enter the full name of the Person or Organization you create a Profile ppage for.

help.helpOptions: And to go further, use these resources...

dashboard.banner: Welcome to 4SEO! View our Getting Started video for a quick tour
dashboard.contactSupport: Please check log files or contact support for help.
dashboard.errorCrawler: The crawler is not working at the moment.
dashboard.linksInError: Some links trigger errors, use Errors | Broken links to locate and fix them.
dashboard.pagesInError: Some pages trigger errors, use Errors | Recorded errors to locate and fix them.
dashboard.errorCrawlerDetails: The 4SEO crawler is responsible for analyzing your site pages. It is in a state of error at the moment.
dashboard.warningInsertAutoCanonical: Automatic canonical link is disabled, enable it back in Pages settings to avoid duplicate content risks.
dashboard.warningMetaAutoDescIfMissing: Automatic meta description is disabled, enable it back in Pages settings for more control on descriptions in search results.
dashboard.noAnalytics: You don't seem to be using any Analytics provider. Consider adding one to measure progress.
dashboard.warningSdDisabled: Automatic insertion of structured data is disabled, enable and configure it back in the Structured Data settings to increase your SEO performance.
dashboard.warningSdMissingLogo: Structured data for Local Business is enabled, but you have not selected a logo, which is required. Please complete structured data configuration in the Structured Data settings.
dashboard.warningSitemapsNotAddingRobotsTxt: Sitemaps are created but not added to your robots.txt. Enable listing your sitemap in your robots.txt file in the Sitemaps settings.
dashboard.warningSitemapsNotSubmitting: Sitemaps are created but not automatically submitted to search engines when ready or updated. Enable auto-submission in the Sitemaps settings.
dashboard.resetRequired: An analysis data reset is required. Reset button is located at Pages | Settings | Site Analysis.
dashboard.resetReasonUpdate: An update was performed that requires previous analysis data to be reset and rebuilt.
dashboard.canImportFromSh404sef: 4SEO has detected that sh404SEF is running on this site. 4SEO can import some relevant data from sh404SEF. Use the Import from sh404SEF section on the Extensions configuration page to do so.
dashboard.gscPromptDetails:
The Search Console provides very useful information about how your site appears in Google Search results. It runs on Google servers and nothing is added to your website.

Using the Google Seach Console is entirely free of charge.

You only need to create an account at the Google Search Console website (if you do not have one already).

With an account, go to 4SEO Configuration | Integrations page. 4SEO will connect to your Search Console account automatically and configure it for you.
dashboard.robotOutAiBotsPrompt: AI bots may use your content for AI training. Block them out with one click if you do not want that.
dashboard.robotOutAiBotsPromptDetails: AI companies such as OpenAI, makers of ChatGPT, have robots that crawl websites to harvest content used in training their AI systems. This may be good or bad for you. You can block common AI robots on the Robots.txt page using the Exclude common AI bots button.
dashboard.profilePagePrompt: Apply Profile Page structured data to your About us or author pages.
dashboard.profilePagePromptDetails: The ProfilePage type of structured data is specifically designed to help search engines understand who the person or organization writing content is. Use a Structured Data rule applied to the desired pages.
dashboard.ugaDeprecationDetails: You appear to be using Google Universal Analytics which will be shut down on July 1st, 2024. We suggest switching to another analytics tool as soon as possible.

configWizard.titleCanonicalRoot: What is your website main address?
configWizard.introText1: 4SEO installed successfully, thank you for using it!
configWizard.introText2: Before getting really started, we have 2 questions for you.
configWizard.introPressNext: Press the Next button when ready.
configWizard.introRootUrl: Most websites should only be accessed at one address. 4SEO can optimize better if it knows your preferred site address.
configWizard.introPublic1: If your website is not public, 4SEO won't tell search engines about it.
configWizard.introPublic2: When your website is ready, you can change this in 4SEO settings.
configWizard.siteIsPublicCaption: If set to Yes, 4SEO will inform search engines that your sitemap exists and will optionally send the sitemap to them directly.
configWizard.thankYou1: 4SEO is now analyzing your site in the background as visitors browse your pages.
configWizard.thankYou2: If your site has no visits yet, you can perform a manual analysis from the Pages page.
configWizard.closeLabel: Close wizard and go to dashboard.
configWizard.gscExplain: You should connect 4SEO to the Google Search Console
configWizard.gscExplain2: The Search Console provides very useful information about how your site appears in Google Search results. It runs on Google servers and nothing is added to your website
configWizard.gscExplain3: Using the Google Seach Console is entirely free of charge.
configWizard.gscExplain4: You only need to create an account at the Google Search Console website (if you do not have one already)
configWizard.gscExplain5: With an account, go to 4SEO Configuration | Integrations page. 4SEO will connect to your Search Console account automatically and configure it for you

importWizard.sh404sefDetected: 4SEO has detected that sh404SEF is running on this site.
importWizard.sh404sefCanImportData: 4SEO can import some relevant data from sh404SEF.
importWizard.sh404sefCannotImportData: 4SEO can import data from sh404SEF but sh404SEF was not detected, or no data that sh404SEF can import was found. sh404SEF must be enabled to allow import. Alternatively, you can use 4SEF to replace sh404SEF
importWizard.sh404sefImportInstructions: Use the Import from sh404SEF section on the Extensions configuration page to do so
importWizard.startWizardCaption: Click the button to start an interactive wizard and select which data should be imported.
importWizard.promptImportMetaText: 4SEO has found sh404SEF meta data record(s) that may be imported into 4SEO. Found %1 record(s) in total
importWizard.promptImportAliasesText: 4SEO has found sh404SEF aliases (redirects or canonicals) record(s) that may be imported into 4SEO. Found %1 record(s) in total
importWizard.wantToImport: Do you want to import them into 4SEO?
importWizard.finalNoImport: Import cancelled, neither meta data nor aliases were imported
importWizard.finalAborted: Import aborted mid-way, meta data or aliases may have been imported entirely or partially. You should probably start over
importWizard.finalRunLater: You can run this importer again at any time from the Import from sh404SEF section on the Extensions configuration page
importWizard.finalStillOffline:
The site is still offline.
Make sure everything works as expected, then set the site back online using Joomla global configuration.
importWizard.finalAliasesImportCompleted: %1 redirect and canonical element(s) were imported.
importWizard.speedModeCaption: Reduce speed if import fails with timeout errors.
importWizard.explainBeforeClearAliasesDb: Any previously imported redirect or canonical in 4SEO will be deleted
importWizard.explainBeforeMeta: All meta data found in sh404SEF will be imported
importWizard.explainBeforeAliases: All redirects and canonicals found in sh404SEF will be imported
importWizard.explainBeforeQuestion: Do you want to proceed with the import?

aliases.listOfAliasesCaption: Enter simple URLs that should redirect to the current page you are editing. Enter one URL per line.
aliases.aliasesSpecWildcard: You cannot use wildcard characters here ({*} or {?}). If you need wildcard characters, use a Redirect rule instead.
aliases.aliasesSpec: Must start with a /, but cannot be just /.
aliases.someAliasesAlreadyUsed: Some of the aliases you entered already redirect to other pages.
aliases.someAliasesAlreadyUsedDetails: They have been ignored. Please reload the page to see the current aliases for this page. Here is the list of already existing aliases:
aliases.noAliasPrompt: No alias defined yet. Aliases are a kind of redirects, simpler that full rules. You can add aliases directly to any page listed on the Pages page.
aliases.creationHelp: Aliases are created by adding them on the Aliases tab of any page on the Pages list.
